description: Full-stack developer working with MongoDB, Express.js, React, and Node.js. Responsible for building and maintaining web applications across the entire stack — from database schemas and RESTful APIs to responsive React UIs and cloud deployment.

responsibilities: Build UI components in React under guidance of senior developers
Write basic API endpoints in Express.js
Fix bugs reported by QA team in assigned modules
Write unit tests for new code
Attend daily standups and sprint planning
Document code and API changes
Own feature development from backend to frontend end-to-end
Design MongoDB schemas and optimize queries
Implement authentication and authorization flows
Review pull requests of junior developers
Refactor legacy code and improve test coverage
Collaborate with designers on UI/UX implementation
Write technical specifications for features
Mentor junior developers on best practices
requirements: Advanced React patterns (context, reducers, custom hooks)

responsibilities: Architect full-stack solutions for complex business requirements
Lead technical decision-making for the team
Design database schemas with performance and scale in mind
Set up monitoring, logging, and alerting infrastructure
Conduct system design reviews and architecture discussions
Drive test automation strategy and code quality standards
Lead incident response and postmortem analysis
Interview candidates and evaluate technical skills
Define technical roadmap and architecture vision
Drive cross-team technical initiatives
Establish engineering standards and best practices organization-wide
Design system for high availability and disaster recovery
Evaluate and select third-party services and infrastructure
Guide career growth of engineering team members
Present technical strategy to C-suite stakeholders
Contribute to open-source and engineering blog

day_to_day: Morning standup (15 min): Share yesterday's progress, today's plan, and any blockers
Code review session: Review 2-3 pull requests from teammates before lunch
Feature development: Work on assigned user stories — write backend logic, then frontend UI, then integration tests
Debugging session: Investigate production issue reported via Sentry — reproduce locally, identify root cause, deploy fix
Technical discussion with team: Whiteboard architecture decisions for upcoming feature
Client call (weekly): Demo new features, gather feedback, clarify requirements
Write documentation: API docs (Swagger), README updates, architecture decision records (ADRs)
Sprint ceremonies: Planning (week start), retro (week end), backlog grooming (mid-week)
Self-learning: 1 hour blocked for reading tech blogs, trying new tools, or contributing to internal tooling

steps: Receive requirement document from PM via Jira ticket
Review acceptance criteria and ask clarifying questions
Design database schema for orders, payments, invoices in MongoDB
Create Express.js routes for payment initiation, webhook handling, refunds
Integrate with payment gateway SDK (Razorpay/Stripe)
Build React checkout form with validation, loading states, error handling
Write unit tests for payment calculation logic
Write integration tests for payment webhook end-to-end flow
Create API documentation for the payment endpoints
Submit PR, get reviewed, address feedback, merge to staging
Deploy to staging, test with test mode payment gateway
After QA sign-off, deploy to production with feature flag
Monitor Sentry for errors in the first 24 hours post-launch
PagerDuty alert fires at 2 AM — Node.js process memory > 90%
Check Grafana dashboard — see memory leak pattern over last 4 hours
SSH into production instance, take heap snapshot using `node --heapsnapshot`
Load heap snapshot in Chrome DevTools Memory tab
Identify that event listeners on WebSocket connections are not being cleaned up
Reproduce locally with load testing script using k6
Fix: Remove event listener leak in socket handler, add memory monitoring log
Write regression test that simulates 1000 concurrent connections
Create PR, get emergency review approval, deploy hotfix to production
Monitor memory metrics — confirm stabilization
Write incident postmortem: root cause, impact, fix, prevention measures
Product team reports analytics dashboard takes 8+ seconds to load
Profile frontend with React DevTools — identify unnecessary re-renders
Profile backend with clinic.js — identify slow MongoDB aggregation query
Run `explain()` on the aggregation — see full collection scan (no index)
Create compound indexes on frequently filtered/grouped fields
Restructure aggregation pipeline — filter early, project only needed fields
Add caching layer with Redis for dashboard data (TTL: 5 minutes)
Implement React lazy loading and code splitting for dashboard components
Use React.memo and useMemo to prevent re-renders on data transitions
Load test with k6 — verify dashboard loads under 800ms with 100 concurrent users

common_issues.symptom: Users get logged out randomly mid-session. API returns 401 intermittently.
common_issues.root_cause: Access token expiry not handled gracefully on frontend. Refresh token endpoint fails silently.
common_issues.fix: Implement axios interceptor that catches 401, calls /refresh-token, retries original request with new token. Queue pending requests during refresh to prevent race conditions.
common_issues.prevention: Set access token TTL to 15 min, refresh token TTL to 7 days. Store refresh token in httpOnly cookie, access token in memory.

common_issues.symptom: API becomes unresponsive, MongoDB logs show 'too many connections'. Requests hang until timeout.
common_issues.root_cause: Default Mongoose connection pool size (100) may be too low for high concurrency, or connections are not being released due to long queries.
common_issues.fix: Increase pool size to match workload (`mongoose.connect(uri, { maxPoolSize: 200 })`). Add connection timeout and retry logic. Profile slow queries causing connection hold.
common_issues.prevention: Monitor connection pool utilization, set up alerts at 80% usage. Implement request timeouts at the Express level.

common_issues.issue: React State Management with Complex Forms
common_issues.symptom: Form inputs lag, state updates are lost, or form re-renders cause performance issues.
common_issues.root_cause: Using useState for every field causes full form re-render on every keystroke. Object spread in setState creates stale closures.
common_issues.fix: Use React Hook Form with field-level subscriptions. Use useReducer for complex state. Debounce validation. Use form libraries with built-in performance optimizations.
common_issues.prevention: Design forms with isolated field components. Use controlled inputs with care. Memoize expensive computations with useMemo.

common_issues.symptom: Frontend can't make API calls — browser shows CORS error in console. Works in dev but breaks in production.
common_issues.root_cause: CORS configuration in Express doesn't match the actual frontend origin. Differences between localhost + port in dev vs custom domain in production.
common_issues.fix: Set CORS dynamically based on NODE_ENV. Use whitelist pattern. Add explicit allowed headers and methods. Handle preflight OPTIONS requests correctly.
common_issues.prevention: Use environment variable for allowed origins. Test CORS in CI with actual production domain. Add proper error responses for CORS failures.

common_issues.symptom: File uploads fail silently, or server crashes when large files are uploaded. Upload progress not visible to user.
common_issues.root_cause: Express body-parser default limit (1MB) or nginx client_max_body_size not configured. No file size validation on frontend.
common_issues.fix: Configure multer with size limits, file type validation, and disk/cloud storage. Implement presigned URL uploads directly to S3 to offload server. Show upload progress with axios onUploadProgress.
common_issues.prevention: Set global upload size limit in nginx (client_max_body_size). Validate file type and size on client before upload. Use chunked upload for large files.

common_issues.symptom: Server becomes slow or unresponsive under sudden traffic spikes. Automated scripts are scraping data without restriction.
common_issues.root_cause: No rate limiting middleware implemented. All endpoints are equally accessible without throttling.
common_issues.fix: Implement express-rate-limit with tiered limits: 100 req/min for auth, 1000 req/min for general API, 10/min for login attempts. Use RedisStore for distributed rate limiting across multiple instances.
common_issues.prevention: Set rate limits from day one. Use different limits for authenticated vs unauthenticated users. Return proper 429 responses with Retry-After headers.

common_issues.symptom: Socket.io connections drop randomly. Users miss live updates. Reconnection causes duplicate messages.
common_issues.root_cause: No heartbeat mechanism. Reconnection logic missing or incorrect. Behind load balancer without sticky sessions.
common_issues.fix: Implement ping/pong heartbeat every 25 seconds. Enable Socket.io reconnection with exponential backoff. Configure load balancer for sticky sessions or use Redis adapter.
common_issues.prevention: Test WebSocket stability under network disruptions. Use Socket.io built-in reconnection. Monitor connection state transitions.

common_issues.symptom: Container restarts randomly. 'OOMKilled' in docker logs. Application becomes progressively slower over time.
common_issues.root_cause: Node.js process has memory leak — global variables, growing cache, unclosed connections, or event emitter listeners accumulating.
common_issues.fix: Take heap dump before restart. Use Chrome DevTools Memory tab to analyze. Fix identified leak sources. Add --max-old-space-size flag to Node.js. Set Docker memory limits.
common_issues.prevention: Add memory usage monitoring to Grafana. Set up alerts for memory growth patterns. Run load tests weekly to catch regressions. Use tools like clinic.js for automatic leak detection.

industry_standards: RESTful API design principles — consistent resource naming, proper HTTP methods, status codes, HATEOAS
JWT-based authentication with access/refresh token pattern and httpOnly cookies
MVC architecture pattern — models handle data, controllers handle logic, views handle presentation
Git feature branch workflow with conventional commits (feat:, fix:, chore:, docs:)
CI/CD pipeline with automated linting, testing, and deployment using GitHub Actions
Code review process requiring at least one approval before merge to main branch
Semantic versioning (semver) — major.minor.patch for breaking, feature, and fix releases
OpenAPI/Swagger documentation for all API endpoints — auto-generated from JSDoc comments
Error handling with centralized error middleware — consistent error response format across all endpoints
Logging with structured JSON format — log level, timestamp, request ID, user context, stack trace
Environment-based configuration — .env files with different configs for dev, staging, production
Twelve-factor app methodology — declarative setup, disposability, dev/prod parity, concurrency
Database schema versioning with migration scripts — avoid manual schema changes on production
Accessibility (a11y) standards — WCAG 2.1 AA compliance for all UI components
Performance budgets — lighthouse scores, bundle size limits, API response time SLAs

tech_stack_expectations.expectations: Full-stack generalist who handles everything from UI to deployment. Fast iteration, minimal process. Ownership of entire features. Comfortable with ambiguity — requirements change weekly. Must ship fast and fix fast. Often expected to do DevOps, QA, and customer support too.
tech_stack_expectations.tech_preferences: Vite + React, Tailwind CSS, MongoDB Atlas, Vercel/Railway, simple CI/CD
tech_stack_expectations.team_dynamics: Flat hierarchy, direct communication with founders, quick decision-making

tech_stack_expectations.expectations: Specialized in frontend or backend with ability to work across the stack. Defined processes (sprints, standups, retros). Cross-team collaboration. Code reviews and technical specs required. Mentorship of juniors expected for senior roles.
tech_stack_expectations.tech_preferences: TypeScript, Next.js, AWS/GCP, microservices emerging, Docker, PostgreSQL + MongoDB
tech_stack_expectations.team_dynamics: Engineering manager + tech lead structure, quarterly planning, cross-functional squads

tech_stack_expectations.expectations: Deep specialization in one area (frontend, backend, or infrastructure). Heavy process — documentation, compliance, security reviews. Multiple approval gates for deployments. Working with legacy code and migration strategies. Stakeholder management and cross-department communication.
tech_stack_expectations.tech_preferences: TypeScript, GraphQL, Kubernetes, internal tooling, monorepo (Nx/Turborepo), feature flags
tech_stack_expectations.team_dynamics: Multiple layers of management, annual planning cycles, matrix organization structure

tech_stack_expectations.expectations: Strong CS fundamentals — algorithms, data structures, system design. Multiple rounds of technical interviews. Code quality and testing are non-negotiable. Scale mindset — code serves millions of users. Data-driven decision making. Technical document writing is a core skill.
tech_stack_expectations.tech_preferences: Custom internal frameworks and tools, monorepos, strict coding standards, formal design reviews
tech_stack_expectations.team_dynamics: Staff+ engineering track, performance reviews with defined levels (L3-L8), OKR-driven

project_types.name: E-Commerce Platform
project_types.description: Full-featured online store with product catalog, cart, checkout, payment, order tracking, and admin panel
challenges: Handling high traffic during sales events

project_types.name: SaaS Dashboard & Analytics
project_types.description: Multi-tenant web application with user management, role-based access, dashboards, and data visualization

project_types.name: Social / Community Platform
project_types.description: User-generated content platform with profiles, feeds, messaging, notifications, and moderation tools

project_types.name: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP)
project_types.description: Internal business management tool covering HR, finance, inventory, CRM, and project management modules

agile_practices: Two-week sprints with planning on Day 1 and retro on Day 10
Daily 15-minute standups — what I did, what I'll do, blockers
Backlog refinement session every Wednesday to groom upcoming stories
Sprint demo on last day of sprint to showcase completed work

code_review_process.before_submitting: Self-review diff, run linter + tests locally, write PR description with context and screenshots
review_criteria: Correctness — does it solve the problem?
Testing — are there tests covering the change?
Security — any exposed data or injection risks?
code_review_process.after_review: Address all comments, re-request review, squash commits, merge with merge commit (not rebase)

deployment_workflow.dev: Auto-deploy on PR creation to preview environment (Vercel/Railway)
deployment_workflow.staging: Auto-deploy on merge to staging branch — run full test suite + smoke tests
deployment_workflow.production: Manual approval required. Feature flags control rollout. Canary deploy to 10% of users, monitor 30 min, then full rollout.

architecture_patterns.pros: Simple to develop and deploy, single codebase, easy debugging
architecture_patterns.cons: Scaling requires scaling entire app, deployment affects all features
architecture_patterns.best_for: MVP, startups with small teams, internal tools

architecture_patterns.pros: Independent scaling, team autonomy, technology diversity
architecture_patterns.cons: Complex deployment, network latency, data consistency challenges
architecture_patterns.best_for: Large teams, complex domains, high-scale applications

architecture_patterns.pros: No server management, auto-scaling, pay-per-use pricing
architecture_patterns.cons: Cold starts, execution time limits, vendor lock-in
architecture_patterns.best_for: Event-driven workloads, irregular traffic patterns, startups

architecture_patterns.pros: Optimized API per client type, reduces over-fetching, simpler frontend code
architecture_patterns.cons: Duplication across BFFs, additional service to maintain

company_types.expectations: Full-stack generalist, fast-paced, own features end-to-end, wear multiple hats (dev + ops + support)
Client-facing communication, multiple projects simultaneously, quick delivery, adherence to client tech preferences
Specialized in frontend or backend, cross-team collaboration, documentation and process adherence
Deep specialization, architectural documentation, compliance and security awareness, mentoring juniors
CS fundamentals, system design skills, data-driven culture, performance review cycles, formal career levels
